paper.title: Moreno-Perez - 2022 - LaLiga Lockdown Conditioning Strategy and Adaptation to In-Game Regulations during COVID-19 Pandemi.pdf
paper.fileName: Moreno-Perez - 2022 - LaLiga Lockdown Conditioning Strategy and Adaptation to In-Game Regulations during COVID-19 Pandemi.pdf

operatorDecision.guidance: Use exactly 2 lines for multiline fields in this paper: line 1 = pre-suspension / first 27 matchdays, line 2 = post-resumption / final 11 matchdays. Keep that same order across all tabs. Only report medical-attention and time-loss separately if the paper reports them separately; otherwise reuse the total. If contusion is unspecified, code it as superficial contusion, not muscle contusion. Use diagnosis rows only for specific diagnoses not already captured by the structured rows. If a location is too vague or combines multiple listed locations, leave the structured row blank and treat it as a note instead; hip/groin can go under hip. Do not infer illness data, burden, or severity values that are not explicitly reported.

injuryOutcome:injuryPlayersCompletedStud.label: Number of players that completed the study

injuryOutcome:injuryTeamsCompletedStudy.label: Number of teams that completed the study

chunks.label: Injury outcome
chunks.description: Extract the full injury outcome section in a single pass.

draftFields.sourceQuote:
In the period before the suspension of the competition due to the COVID-19 pandemic (i.e., the first 27 matchdays), there were a total of 249 injuries recorded in the teams under investigation.
After the resumption of the competition, there were a total of 69 injuries
draftFields.label: Total number of injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: A total of 277 professional football players (mean ± SD age: 27.7 ± 4.1 years; height: 180.3 ± 7.9 cm; body mass: 75.7 ± 6.4 kg) from 11 teams competing in LaLiga participated in this investigation.
draftFields.label: Number of players that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote: A total of 277 professional football players ... from 11 teams competing in LaLiga participated in this investigation.
draftFields.label: Number of teams that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote:
In the period before the suspension of the competition due to the COVID-19 pandemic (i.e., the first 27 matchdays), there were a total of 249 injuries recorded in the teams under investigation.
After the resumption of the competition, there were a total of 69 injuries
draftFields.label: Total medical attention injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote:
In the period before the suspension of the competition due to the COVID-19 pandemic (i.e., the first 27 matchdays), there were a total of 249 injuries recorded in the teams under investigation.
After the resumption of the competition, there were a total of 69 injuries
draftFields.label: Total time-loss injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote:
Table 2. Conditions of injury in professional football players for the matchdays performed before the suspension of LaLiga due to the COVID-19 pandemic and after the resumption of the competition.
Competition Before Suspension: 126 (50.6)
Competition After Resumption: 44 (60.9)
draftFields.label: Total match injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote:
Table 2. Conditions of injury in professional football players for the matchdays performed before the suspension of LaLiga due to the COVID-19 pandemic and after the resumption of the competition.
Training Before Suspension: 123 (49.4)

draftFields.sourceQuote:
This represented an overall injury incidence of 4.2 injuries per 1000 h of exposure (range = 1.9–7.7 injuries per 1000 h of exposure).
After the resumption of the competition, there were a total of 69 injuries and the injury incidence was similar to before the competition suspension (5.4; 1.5–9.6 injuries per 1000 h of exposure, p = 0.338).
draftFields.label: Injury incidence (overall)

draftFields.sourceQuote:
Table 1. Incidence of injuries shown according to tissue damage and time of injury (in training or during the match) in professional football players for the matchdays played before the suspension of LaLiga due to the COVID-19 pandemic and after the resumption of the competition.
Competition Before Suspension: 25.9 (11.2-38.2)
Competition After Resumption: 21.5 (5.5-49.6)
draftFields.label: Match injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote:
Table 1. Incidence of injuries shown according to tissue damage and time of injury (in training or during the match) in professional football players for the matchdays played before the suspension of LaLiga due to the COVID-19 pandemic and after the resumption of the competition.
Training Before Suspension: 2.3 (0.9-4.6)
Training After Resumption: 2.5 (0.0-4.0)
draftFields.label: Training injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote: Muscle/tendon was the most commonly injured tissue before suspension and after the resumption of competition.
draftFields.label: Most common injury type

draftFields.sourceQuote: The distribution of injuries according to body location was not affected by the suspension of the championship, with lower limb being the most common in both scenarios.
draftFields.label: Most common injury location

draftFields.sourceQuote:
Table 2. Conditions of injury in professional football players for the matchdays performed before the suspension of LaLiga due to the COVID-19 pandemic and after the resumption of the competition.
Severity Before Suspension: 8-28 days 113 (45.4)
Severity After Resumption: 1-7 days 32 (46.4)
draftFields.label: Most common injury severity class

draftFields.sourceQuote:
